extracted_codes.context: We selected all individuals with at least 1 SLE ICD-9 code (710.0), yielding 5,959 individuals

clinical_criteria: 3 or more counts of the SLE ICD-9 code (710.0)
Ever use of both disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs (DMARDs) and steroids
Diagnosed with SLE by a rheumatologist, nephrologist, or dermatologist
Exclusion of individuals with systemic sclerosis and dermatomyositis ICD-9 codes
algorithm_summary: The SLE phenotype algorithm identifies patients using a combination of: (1) 3 or more counts of SLE ICD-9 code 710.0, (2) positive ANA test (≥1:40), (3) ever use of both DMARDs and corticosteroids, and (4) exclusion of patients with systemic sclerosis and dermatomyositis ICD-9 codes. Additional components evaluated include the keyword 'lupus' in problem lists and use of antimalarials. The highest performing algorithm achieved 95% PPV in training set and 91% PPV in validation set. Cases were defined as patients diagnosed with SLE by specialists (rheumatologists, nephrologists, or dermatologists), excluding cutaneous lupus and drug-induced lupus.
